frameworks.name: Bayesian observer model
frameworks.one_line: Speed perception modeled as prior combined with likelihood via Bayes rule

frameworks.name: efficient coding
frameworks.one_line: Stimulus distribution jointly constrains prior and likelihood

core_question: Can efficient-coding constraints yield a better-identified Bayesian observer model of visual speed perception?
key_assumptions: Observer combines prior with likelihood via Bayes rule
Stimulus distribution links and jointly constrains likelihood and prior
Discrimination thresholds and biases reflect a single normative model
manipulations: Fit model across a broad range of stimulus speeds
Fit model across multiple stimulus uncertainty levels
Cross-validated against parametric per-curve psychometric approximations
key_findings: Constrained model fits discrimination data as well as parametric per-curve approximations
Extracted prior follows a power-law function with exponent near -1
Recovered prior is much more consistent across subjects than under a previous model
interpretations: Efficient-coding constraint resolves identifiability problems of the Bayesian framework for speed perception
A low-speed power-law prior is a robust, subject-invariant feature of visual speed estimation
limitations_noted: Framework flexibility makes Bayesian models hard to validate without added constraints
